title: Delivery Overview — Orvex Studio
what_it_is: The programme-level 'Delivery Overview' for all of Orvex Studio (Houston-pattern adaptation, ratified CANONICAL 2026-07-06). It defines a flat, single-track roadmap of 15 Linear Project Milestones (M0–M14) grouped into dependency waves (Wave 0 contracts/lib-spine → Wave A identity/workflows/billing/knowledge → Wave B engine/wiki-tier/AI/MCP → Wave C BFF/SPA/CLI/console/launch) with fixed target dates (2026-07-14 through 2026-09-29), executed directly via the Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator (gkkUDzn277) and the Issue Authoring Prompt (9VUHxAcoXw). The rest of the page is reusable live-dashboard machinery — Linear-embed 'Program status'/'All work' views, a 'Milestone dashboards' subpage index driven off the GG6RzEvtWh template, and a live 'Frontier' block — all populated by docmost-cli page block embeds rather than hand-typed numbers.
overlaps: Directly conflicts with the current model of record, the 4-phase Delivery Program 5eFdxN3edd (Phase 0 Stabilize → Phase 1 Definition Factory → Phase 2 Isolated Builds → Phase 2.5/3), executed via yXUWpQpRjx/Ng66su4dVG/ErYdXzIj6g. This page's flat Wave 0/A/B/C → M0–M14 roadmap, ratified a week before the phase program existed, has no Foundation-stabilize phase, no Definition Factory, no contract-tag-unlock isolated-build structure and no Product-Acceptance-E2E gate — it assumes the whole programme runs straight through Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator waves with fixed calendar dates, which the phase model has since replaced (same underlying gkkUDzn277 + UKanXYLCQD machinery, different sequencing wrapper). A reader hitting this CANONICAL page would reasonably believe the wave/M0–M14 roadmap is still the plan, not the 4-phase one. It also overlaps at the 'what's actually blocking delivery' layer with cjv9Q4RuyI (Studio Family Foundation Rollup, DATED-SNAPSHOT) and will collide with the new parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service / contract-tag unlock, still local-only) once that lands — three competing pictures of 'how Studio gets built' otherwise coexist. Recommended fold: retire the wave/M0–M14 roadmap table and target-date claims (superseded by 5eFdxN3edd's phase structure), but carry the reusable live-dashboard machinery (project-scoped Linear embeds, Milestone Dashboard template/subpage index, 'Frontier' ready-now block, Linear project/team IDs) into 5eFdxN3edd (or a child status page under it) as its live tracking layer so that setup isn't lost or duplicated.
